title: Genomic DNA Extraction Protocol using DNeasy® UltraClean® Microbial Kit
author: Md Sahadat Ali

steps.title: of the culture, ensuring it is around 0
steps.instruction: of the culture, ensuring it is around 0.7 for optimal DNA yield. Transfer 1.8 mL of bacterial culture (grown in LB broth for 24 hours) into a 2 mL collection tube. Centrifuge at 10,000 x g for 30 seconds. Carefully decant the supernatant. Repeat centrifugation (10,000 x g, 30 seconds) and remove the remaining supernatant using a pipette. Note

steps.title: provides optimal DNA yield and purity
steps.instruction: provides optimal DNA yield and purity. Note

steps.title: Longer centrifugation times may be required...
steps.instruction: Longer centrifugation times may be required to achieve an adequate pellet. Note

steps.title: It is recommended to perform this...
steps.instruction: It is recommended to perform this step inside a biosafety cabinet to maintain sterility and prevent contamination.

steps.title: Cell Lysis Resuspend the cell pellet...
steps.instruction: Cell Lysis Resuspend the cell pellet in 300 µL of PowerBead® Solution and vortex gently. Transfer the resuspended cells to a PowerBead® Tube. Add 50 µL of Solution SL to the PowerBead® Tube. If precipitated, preheat Solution SL at 55°C before use. Note: Solution SL contains SDS and disruption agents to break and open bacterial cells. 4. Secure the PowerBead® Tubes horizontally in the vortex adapter and vortex at maximum speed for 10 minutes. Note: This step ensures efficient mechanical and chemical lysis of cells.

steps.title: Removing Cell Debris Ensure PowerBead® tubes...
steps.instruction: Removing Cell Debris Ensure PowerBead® tubes rotate freely in the centrifuge to prevent damage. Centrifuge at 10,000 x g for 30 seconds. Transfer the supernatant (~300–350 µL) to a clean 2 mL collection tube. Note: The pellet contains cell debris, while the DNA remains in the supernatant.

steps.title: Inhibitor Removal Add 100 µL of...
steps.instruction: Inhibitor Removal Add 100 µL of Inhibitor Removal Solution (IRS) to the supernatant. Vortex for 5 seconds. Incubate at 4°C for 5 minutes. Centrifuge at 10,000 x g for 1 minute. Carefully transfer the supernatant to a new 2 mL collection tube, avoiding the pellet. Note: The pellet contains non-DNA organic and inorganic contaminants.

steps.title: Load 700 µL of the mixture...
steps.instruction: Load 700 µL of the mixture into an MB Spin Column.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 10,000 x g for 30 seconds
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 10,000 x g for 30 seconds.

steps.title: Repeat steps 18–20 until the entire...
steps.instruction: Repeat steps 18–20 until the entire sample is processed (usually 2–3 loads).

steps.title: Washing & Purification Add 300 µL...
steps.instruction: Washing & Purification Add 300 µL of Column Binding (CB) Solution to the MB Spin Column. Centrifuge at 10,000 x g for 30 seconds. Note: CB solution removes residual salts and contaminants. 3. Discard the flow-through and centrifuge again for 1 minute at 10,000 x g to remove residual CB solution.

steps.title: DNA Elution Transfer the MB Spin...
steps.instruction: DNA Elution Transfer the MB Spin Column to a new 2 mL collection tube. Add 50 µL of autoclaved Double DI (DDI) water directly to the center of the white filter membrane instead of Solution EB. Centrifuge at 10,000 x g for 30 seconds to elute the DNA. Discard the MB Spin Column and store the extracted DNA at -80°C. Note 1: The eluted DNA is ready for PCR, sequencing, or other molecular applications. Note 2: Autoclaved Double DI (DDI) water was used instead of Solution EB to elute the DNA, ensuring a nuclease-free environment while avoiding potential salt or EDTA interference in downstream applications.

steps.title: Expected Results High-quality, purified DNA suitable...
steps.instruction: Expected Results High-quality, purified DNA suitable for molecular biology applications such as PCR, sequencing, and cloning. 1. DNA concentration and purity can be measured using: NanoDrop TM spectrophotometer – for absorbance-based quantification and purity assessment (A260/A280 ratio should be ~1.8–2.0 for high-purity DNA). Qubit TM fluorometer – for precise quantification using fluorescent dye-based detection. 2. DNA integrity can be confirmed via agarose gel electrophoresis : Run 5 µL of extracted DNA on a 0.8% agarose gel stained with EtBr or SYBR Safe. High-quality genomic DNA should appear as a sharp, high-molecular-weight band with minimal smearing. Smearing or degraded bands may indicate DNA fragmentation due to poor handling or degradation. Note: If DNA is degraded or of low yield, consider optimizing lysis conditions, increasing sample input, or improving elution efficiency.
